Verbatim_Snippet: 129. (1) A data user shall not transfer any personal data of a data subject to a place outside Malaysia unless to such place as specified by the Minister, upon the recommendation of the Commissioner, by notification published in the Gazette.
Mapping_Rationale: This provision establishes a conditional cross-border data transfer regime by imposing a general prohibition on transferring personal data outside Malaysia. Transfers are only permitted to destinations specified by the Minister via a Gazette notification (typically based on adequacy or similar laws), or under specific statutory exceptions, ensuring a baseline of comparable protection.

Verbatim_Snippet: Malaysia does not have blanket localisation requirements but enforces stricter conditions in financial services, telecommunications, and health (particularly under the supervision of Bank Negara Malaysia and sectoral regulators).
Mapping_Rationale: The provided text indicates that Malaysia does not impose general or blanket data localisation requirements under its primary data protection law. However, it notes that sectoral regulators impose stricter localisation conditions for specific industries such as financial services, telecommunications, and health, meaning local storage is required conditionally based on the sector rather than universally.

Mapping_Rationale: This text describes a sectoral data localization requirement for the financial industry, mandating that Malaysian bank data be stored in domestic data centres under Bank Negara Malaysia's RMiT policy. Although the source is a market research report rather than the primary statute, it confirms the existence of a local infrastructure requirement for regulated financial entities.

Mapping_Rationale: This provision establishes a conditional cross-border data transfer regime. Verification_Actual_Text: 129. (1) A data user shall not transfer any personal data of a data subject to a place outside Malaysia unless to such place as specified by the Minister, upon the recommendation of the Commissioner, by notification published in the Gazette. (2) For the purposes of subsection (1), the Minister may specify any place outside Malaysia if— (a) there is in that place in force any law which is substantially similar to this Act, or that serves the same purposes as this Act; or (b) that place ensures an adequate level of protection
